      • composite(l: OffsetLayer; canvas: Canvas; offset: Offset)
      • composite(l: OpacityLayer; canvas: Canvas; offset: Offset)
      • composite(l: PictureLayer; canvas: Canvas; offset: Offset)
      • composite(l: TransformLayer; canvas: Canvas; offset: Offset)
        compositeBoundary
      • compositeBoundary(canvas: Canvas; sub: Canvas; offset: Offset; size: Size)

Layer tree: a parallel tree to the render tree that captures rasterizable units. Each Layer knows how to paint itself onto a Canvas. The compositor walks the layer tree and either re-paints a layer (when its subtree is dirty) or composites a cached texture from the previous frame (when it isn't).

A RepaintBoundary widget creates a BoundaryLayer that owns its own sub-canvas. The sub-canvas's pixel buffer is rasterized exactly once per change and then composited on every frame. For static subtrees the per-frame cost collapses to one GPU blit.

Design parallels Flutter's dart:ui Layer hierarchy but is intentionally smaller: only the layer types we actually need.

Imports

geometry, render_object

Types

BoundaryLayer = ref object of ContainerLayer
  subCanvas*: Canvas
  size*: Size
  dirty*: bool

A cacheable layer. Owns a sub-canvas onto which its subtree rasterizes. When dirty is false, the cached sub-canvas is composited as-is. When dirty is true the sub-canvas is re-rasterized first.

The actual sub-canvas object is backend-specific. We hold it opaquely as a Canvas ref. The backend that constructed the parent canvas is responsible for vending the sub-canvas and for the composite step.

ClipRectLayer = ref object of ContainerLayer
  clipBounds*: Rect
Clips children to a rectangle. 
ContainerLayer = ref object of Layer
  children*: seq[Layer]
A layer with children. Compositing walks children in order. 
Layer = ref object of RootObj
  parent*: Layer
  offset*: Offset
  needsComposite*: bool
Base type for every node in the layer tree. Concrete layers override composite to put their contents onto a parent canvas. Most layers are container-shaped (children); BoundaryLayer is the cache-bearing leaf-ish kind. 
OffsetLayer = ref object of ContainerLayer
Translates the coordinate system of its children by offset. 
OpacityLayer = ref object of ContainerLayer
  opacity*: float32
Multiplies the alpha of every primitive painted inside. 
PictureLayer = ref object of Layer
  paintFn*: proc (canvas: Canvas; offset: Offset) {.closure.}
  size*: Size
A leaf layer whose contents are drawn by a paintFn callback. Used as a fallback for render objects that don't (yet) build their own specialized layers. 
TransformLayer = ref object of ContainerLayer
  translation*: Offset
  rotation*: float32
  scale*: float32
Applies translate + rotate + scale to children.

Procs

proc add(parent: ContainerLayer; child: Layer) {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Appends child to parent.children and sets child.parent. Safe for child == nil (no-op). 
proc clearChildren(parent: ContainerLayer) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Drops all children. Used when a layer is rebuilt from its render object during paint. Children become unreachable; their cached sub-canvases (if any) get released by the GC. 
proc isDirty(boundary: BoundaryLayer): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Returns true if this boundary needs re-rasterization on this frame. 
proc markBoundaryDirty(layer: Layer) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Walks up the layer chain marking the nearest enclosing BoundaryLayer as dirty. Render objects call this when their paint output would differ from the cached texture. 
proc newBoundaryLayer(size: Size): BoundaryLayer {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Builds a BoundaryLayer of the given size. The subCanvas field is left nil; the backend fills it in via attachSubCanvas when it sees the boundary during paint. 
proc newPictureLayer(paintFn: proc (canvas: Canvas; offset: Offset) {.closure.};
                     size: Size = SizeZero): PictureLayer {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Builds a PictureLayer from a paint closure. Used as a leaf layer for the fallback "just paint normally" path. 
proc paintIntoSubCanvas(boundary: BoundaryLayer;
                        paintFn: proc (sub: Canvas) {.closure.}) {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}

Re-rasterizes the boundary by clearing its sub-canvas and running paintFn against it. Only call when boundary.dirty is true. Resets dirty to false on success.

Inputs:

  • boundary: the cached layer.
  • paintFn: a closure that draws the subtree onto the sub-canvas.

Effect: subtree is rasterized into boundary.subCanvas; future composites can blit directly without redrawing.

Methods

method composite(l: BoundaryLayer; canvas: Canvas; offset: Offset) {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}

If dirty, ask the backend to re-rasterize children into subCanvas (the caller is expected to have done this already via paintIntoBoundary; we just composite). Then composite the sub canvas onto the parent at the given absolute offset.

When subCanvas is nil (no backend support) we fall back to directly painting children onto the parent canvas; the perf win is lost but correctness is preserved.

method composite(l: ClipRectLayer; canvas: Canvas; offset: Offset) {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
method composite(l: ContainerLayer; canvas: Canvas; offset: Offset) {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Default container composite: walks children in order, adding their per-child offset on top of the absolute origin. 
method composite(l: Layer; canvas: Canvas; offset: Offset) {.base, ...raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Paint this layer (and any subtree it owns) onto canvas at the given absolute offset. Subclasses override; the base no-op is useful for test stubs. 
method composite(l: OffsetLayer; canvas: Canvas; offset: Offset) {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Translates by the layer's own offset before descending. 
method composite(l: OpacityLayer; canvas: Canvas; offset: Offset) {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Pushes opacity onto the canvas stack, composites children, pops. 
method composite(l: PictureLayer; canvas: Canvas; offset: Offset) {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Calls the stored paintFn to draw at offset. 
method composite(l: TransformLayer; canvas: Canvas; offset: Offset) {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Applies translate + rotate + scale via canvas state. 
method compositeBoundary(canvas: Canvas; sub: Canvas; offset: Offset; size: Size) {.
    base, ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Backend hook for compositing a BoundaryLayer's sub-canvas onto the parent canvas. Default no-op. Concrete canvases override to implement the GPU-side blit (SDL: upload sub to an SDL texture, then SDL_RenderCopy; embedded: per-pixel image copy).
